_comment: Example /etc/fleet/mcp-policy.json that lets an UNPRIVILEGED MCP client (e.g. Claude run as an ordinary user) deploy and manage SPECIFIC apps through the privilege-separated root daemon — without sudo. read/mutate stay allowed; the destructive tier stays denied by default, and deploy/lifecycle are opted in per-tool AND scoped to named apps via { apps: [...] } so a compromised or prompt-injected agent cannot touch anything off the list. A bare "allow" would permit every registered app. Every call is still rate-limited and written to /var/log/fleet-mcp/audit.log. Copy to /etc/fleet/mcp-policy.json and restart the daemon (sudo systemctl restart fleet-mcp).
